name: Gravitational baryogenesis
summary: A gravitational coupling between the derivative of the Ricci scalar dR/dt and the baryon-number current J_B^mu (term: (1/M^2) (d_mu R) J_B^mu in the action) dynamically breaks CPT in an expanding universe. Combined with B-violating interactions, drives the universe toward an equilibrium baryon asymmetry. Provides a baryogenesis mechanism that does not require Sakharov's out-of-equilibrium condition or sphalerons; works in equilibrium during radiation domination.

addresses_puzzles.note: Reproduces eta ~ 6e-10 for M ~ 10^12-10^16 GeV and B-violating interactions active until T ~ 10^15 GeV. Mechanism is intrinsically gravitational - depends on dR/dt during radiation-matter transition - so the prediction is essentially fixed by the cosmological history once M is set.

touches_benchmarks.note: Required B-violation at high T can be tied to specific GUT-scale operators contributing to proton decay; predictions are model-dependent.

introduces.note: Single gravitational coupling scale M and the underlying B-violating-operator structure.

history: Davoudiasl, Kitano, Kribs, Murayama, Steinhardt (2004), 'Gravitational baryogenesis', Phys. Rev. Lett. 93, 201301, arXiv:hep-ph/0403019. Subsequent generalisations to f(R) gravity, scalar-tensor theories; review activity 2005-2020.

references.citation: Davoudiasl, Kitano, Kribs, Murayama, Steinhardt, 'Gravitational baryogenesis', Phys. Rev. Lett. 93, 201301 (2004)
